canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Vicuna is predominantly described as a benchmark for evaluating the quality of open-ended text generation from language models. It is frequently used to assess model capabilities such as the helpfulness of responses, performance on instruction-following tasks, and the quality of conversational responses. One study also uses Vicuna as a measure of a model's commonsense knowledge. There is some variation in how the benchmark is operationalized: the most common description frames it as being based on "user-shared conversations" or "human-generated instructions from various tasks". A more specific characterization, found in one paper, defines it as a set of "80 challenging questions". Another source notes that the evaluation of conversational responses on this benchmark involves the use of GPT-4 ratings. In several papers, Vicuna is studied alongside other evaluation sets like WizardLM, Koala, and Self-instruct.
papers: Exploring the Hidden Capacity ofLLMs for One-Step Text Generation
Judge and Improve: Towards a Better Reasoning of Knowledge Graphs with Large Language Models
Beyond Imitation: Leveraging Fine-grained Quality Signals for Alignment
MiniLLM: Knowledge Distillation of Large Language Models
Federated Residual Low-Rank Adaption of Large Language Models
LoCA: Location-Aware Cosine Adaptation for Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning

definitions.text: Benchmark based on user-shared conversations to evaluate open-ended text generation quality and helpfulness of model responses.
definitions.paper: Beyond Imitation: Leveraging Fine-grained Quality Signals for Alignment
